obligations.obligation:
Regulation 9(4) of the
Trading Licence Regulations 2020 provides as under:
“(4) The Trading Licensee shall make an appropriate application accompanied
by prescribed fees for up-gradation of its licence to a higher category or down-
gradation of its licence to a lower category in order to fulfil the ...
obligations.short_summary: Applicant must apply for license category change following specified procedure.

obligations.obligation:
Regulation 15(1) of the Trading Licence Regulations 2020 provides as under:
“15(1) Upgradation of licence:
(a) The Trading Licensee may at any time during a particular year apply for a
higher category licence on the basis of the volume proposed to be traded in a
particular Year.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
(b) The Trading Licensee shall file an application before the Commission for
upgradation of its licence to a higher category as soon as he expects the trading
volume to exceed the trading volume limit specified for the Trading Licensee’s
existing category.
obligations.short_summary: Application for license category upgrade required upon exceeding trading volume limits.

obligations.obligation:
(c) The Trading Licensee shall be granted an opportunity of being heard by the
Commission before taking a decision on the application.
obligations.short_summary: Opportunity to be heard before decision on application

obligations.obligation:
(d) The Trading Licensee shall be required to submit the audited special
balance sheet to support the compliance of Net Worth requirement for higher
category licence.
obligations.due_date: within six months from license issuance date or N/A if already submitted
obligations.short_summary: Submission of audited special balance sheet for compliance verification.

obligations.obligation:

obligations.affected_entity: Trading Licensee with upgraded license category
obligations.short_summary: Pay additional fee for higher licence category if previously paid lower-category fees.

obligations.obligation:
As regards the net worth
requirements for Category ‘IV', Regulation 3 (3) (a) of the Trading Licence Regulations,
2020 provides that a person applying for a Category ‘IV’ trading licence should have a
net worth of Rs. 10 crore on the date of the Special Audited Balance Sheet
accompanying the Petitio...
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The
licensee shall remain bound by the terms and conditions contained in the order dated
22.3.2022 in Petition No. 262/TD/2021, whereby it was issued with a Category ‘V’
trading licence.
obligations.short_summary: Must adhere to Category ‘V’ trading license terms.

obligations.obligation:
As per Regulation 3(3) (a) of the Trading Licence Regulations, a Category ‘V’
licensee cannot trade more than 500 MUs in a finance year.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Accordingly, the Petitioner
is directed to pay the licence fee for Category IV for the financial year 2025-26 along
with 1% surcharge as per the provision of the Payment of Fees Regulations, up to
filing of the present Petition, within 15 days from the issuance of the order.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Let an extract copy of this order be sent to the Central Government (Ministry of
Power) and the Central Electricity Authority in terms of sub-section (7) of Section 15
of the Electricity Act, 2003, for their information and record.
